Workflows.title: Production Deployment Workflow
Workflows.description: The primary NTWF ABox example. A 5-step workflow for deploying software to production at Talisman's Special Solutions. Steps involve a human Release Manager (step 1), an AI Risk Analysis Agent (step 2), a human Legal Compliance Reviewer at an Approval Gate (step 3), an automated CI/CD pipeline (step 4), and the Release Manager again for the post-deployment report (step 5). Demonstrates all three agent types, the delegation chain, artifact provenance, and DCAT dataset consumption.

Roles.label: CI/CD Executor
Roles.comment: Executes the automated build, test, and deployment sequence. Filled by an AutomatedPipeline. Demonstrates CQ3: this step does NOT require human approval.

Roles.label: Chief Technology Officer
Roles.comment: Top of the delegation chain. Has cap-executive-authorization. When both Release Manager and VP Engineering are unavailable, the CTO is the final escalation target.

Roles.label: Deployment Health Agent
Roles.comment: Generates the post-deployment health report by summarizing telemetry after a release. A role, not an identity — currently filled by the DeploymentHealth-AI agent (health-summarizer-v1.2.0), it could be reassigned to a human or a newer model without changing the workflow structure. Its RoleAssignments history records an earlier human-reviewer period and the agent-type transitions.

Roles.label: VP of Engineering
Roles.comment: First escalation target when Release Manager is unavailable. Demonstrates ntwf:delegatesTo chain: Release Manager → VP Engineering → CTO.

Roles.label: Risk Analysis Agent
Roles.comment: Performs probabilistic risk scoring over the Q1 risk metrics dataset. This is a role, not an identity — filled by RiskAnalysis-AI today but could be replaced by a newer model version without changing the workflow structure.

Roles.label: Legal Compliance Reviewer
Roles.comment: Certifies that the deployment plan meets regulatory and contractual requirements. Must be a human agent — AI agents may not fill this role (requiresHumanApproval).

Roles.label: Release Manager
Roles.comment: Owns the production deployment process. Fills the Release Approval Gate (step 3): the human authority who explicitly authorizes the release before the automated deployment runs. This is the CQ2 answer — gate -> Release Manager -> Maria Gonzalez. Top of the delegation chain (Release Manager -> VP Engineering -> CTO).

RoleAssignments.reason: compliance reassignment: automated task assigned to a human for execution
model upgrade: returned to automated summarization (health-summarizer-v1.2.0)

AutomatedPipelines.name: CI/CD Deploy Pipeline
AutomatedPipelines.description: Automated build, test, and deployment pipeline for production releases. Deterministic execution — no probabilistic output.

WorkflowStatusConcepts.definition: Workflow is being authored or reviewed and is not yet in production use.
WorkflowStatusConcepts.scopeNote: Set this status when the workflow has not completed its initial review cycle.

WorkflowStatusConcepts.definition: Workflow is approved and currently being executed by the organization.
WorkflowStatusConcepts.scopeNote: Only active workflows should be assigned to running process instances.

WorkflowStatusConcepts.definition: Workflow has been replaced by a newer version and should not be used for new instances.
WorkflowStatusConcepts.scopeNote: Deprecated workflows may still have in-flight instances; do not archive until all instances close.

WorkflowStatusConcepts.definition: Workflow is no longer in use and has been moved to historical records.
WorkflowStatusConcepts.scopeNote: Archived workflows answer CQ5 stale-workflow queries but generate no new instances.

AgentCapabilityConcepts.definition: The filler must be a human agent capable of contextual, ethical, and legally binding decisions.
AgentCapabilityConcepts.scopeNote: Used for ApprovalGate steps where requiresHumanApproval is true. AI agents may not fill roles with this capability.

AgentCapabilityConcepts.definition: The filler must be capable of probabilistic risk scoring over structured datasets.
AgentCapabilityConcepts.scopeNote: Can be satisfied by an AIAgent with an appropriate model version (e.g. risk-classifier-v2.4.1).

AgentCapabilityConcepts.definition: The filler must have authority to certify regulatory and contractual compliance.
AgentCapabilityConcepts.scopeNote: Requires a human agent. Cannot be delegated to AI or automated pipeline.

AgentCapabilityConcepts.definition: The filler must be an automated pipeline capable of running a deterministic build, test, and deploy sequence.
AgentCapabilityConcepts.scopeNote: Satisfied by AutomatedPipelines. HumanAgents should not fill roles requiring only this capability.

AgentCapabilityConcepts.definition: The filler must hold executive authority to approve high-impact organizational changes.
AgentCapabilityConcepts.scopeNote: Reserved for the top of delegation chains. Maps to the ntwf:delegatesTo escalation pattern.

ArtifactTypeConcepts.definition: A human- or AI-authored analytical document (e.g. a risk assessment report).
ArtifactTypeConcepts.scopeNote: Use for narrative analytical artifacts, not for raw datasets or machine logs.

ArtifactTypeConcepts.definition: An artifact recording an approval or authorization decision (e.g. a release authorization, a legal clearance).
ArtifactTypeConcepts.scopeNote: Use for sign-off / clearance / authorization artifacts.

ArtifactTypeConcepts.definition: A machine-generated record of an automated activity (e.g. a deployment log).
ArtifactTypeConcepts.scopeNote: Use for deterministic pipeline/system output, not human analysis.

GovernanceRoles.responsibilities: Monitors the ontology for drift, tracks external-dependency updates (PROV-O, FOAF, Dublin Core, DCAT, Schema.org), fields questions from users, maintains documentation, and keeps the validation suite current. Identifies that a change is needed but does not approve it. Runs the periodic (at minimum quarterly) competency-question review: new unanswerable questions become scope-change requests, obsolete questions become deprecation candidates, and questions that return wrong answers are defects.

Holds the power to approve changes to the CBox, ABox, and TBox. Decides how and where a change is made. Reviews changes proposed by the steward so that no single person alters the knowledge graph's semantic integrity unchecked.

ChangeLog.termsAffected: Initial release: 9 classes, 13 object properties, 5 datatype properties, 2 CBox schemes.
ChangeLog.rationale: First validated release of the NTWF workflow ontology; passes the 104-test suite.

ChangeLog.motivatingQuestion: Which AI agent was executing this step on March 1, 2026?
ChangeLog.termsAffected: Added filledBy assignment history (RoleAssignments) with validity periods; AIAgent deployment date; agent-type-change audit witnesses.
ChangeLog.rationale: Operational governance needed retained filledBy history so historical 'as of date' and blast-radius queries are answerable without discarding provenance. Additive and backward compatible — a minor, backward-compatible change, so the steward is authorized to approve and deploy it without authority review.

VocabularyReconciliations.rationale: Worked example: a deprecated FOAF element is re-prefixed into the NTWF namespace; existing data stays valid (URIs do not disappear), and foaf:name owl:sameAs ntwf:name keeps the two interchangeable for reasoners.

External-dependency fact: DCAT went through a major v2 -> v3 revision that introduced new classes and deprecated others. NTWF tracks the alignment so its dcat:Dataset usage stays semantically current; existing data remains valid because URIs do not disappear.

Scenarios.label: Stale workflow with an AI step
Scenarios.explanation: Backdates the workflow's last review past its 12-month policy window so the docs go STALE. The release already has AI-executed steps, so stale-docs-AND-an-AI-step fires the article's headline business query: IsStaleAndHasAIAgent (CQ5 extended — stale workflows that involve AI agents).

Scenarios.label: All-human release
Scenarios.explanation: Reassigns every AI agent and pipeline to a human. The AI-step count drops to zero, so the compliance risk can no longer fire — even if the docs are stale. Shows the role→agent indirection: personnel change is one edge, not a rewrite.

Scenarios.label: AI at the approval gate
Scenarios.explanation: Hands the Legal Compliance Reviewer role — whose review step requires human sign-off — to an AI agent. The reasoner's consistency witness fires on that step: a requires-human-approval step is no longer human-filled. The rule break is DERIVED, not hand-checked.

Scenarios.label: Reset to baseline
Scenarios.explanation: Restores the canonical seed assignments and a fresh review date — the known-good starting point. Every role is filled by exactly one agent, no requires-human-approval step is AI-filled, and the workflow is within its staleness window, so all the article's clean-ABox witnesses read green.

Scenarios.label: Re-route the last ordering edge
Scenarios.explanation: Re-points the final precedence edge so step 5 follows step 1 instead of step 4. The transitive closure shrinks (10 → 7 ordered pairs) and the inferred positions recompute — nothing else moves. Demonstrates CQ1: order is a projection of the edges.

Scenarios.label: AI fills the release gate
Scenarios.explanation: Hands the Release Manager role — the human authority on the approval gate — to an AI agent. The gate approver is no longer human (CQ2), and because that role also executes step 3, the AI-step count ticks up (CQ3). The two answers cannot be decoupled: the gate's approver IS a step executor.

Scenarios.label: Hand the risk step to a human
Scenarios.explanation: Reassigns the Risk Analysis role from its AI agent to a human. The AI-executed step count drops from 2 to 1 — and only that. Demonstrates CQ3: the count is a live rollup over which steps are AI-executed, not a stored number.

Scenarios.label: Break the provenance chain
Scenarios.explanation: Clears the wasDerivedFrom link on the Legal Clearance Certificate. The PROV derivation chain splits in two — the downstream artifacts can no longer trace back to the risk report. Demonstrates CQ4: derivation is an asserted edge, and the chain answer follows it.

Scenarios.label: Let the docs go stale
Scenarios.explanation: Backdates the workflow's last-modified date past its staleness window. IsStale flips to true — and only that derived flag. Demonstrates CQ5: staleness is MonthsSinceModified vs the threshold, recomputed against the clock, never asserted.

Scenarios.label: Cut the escalation chain
Scenarios.explanation: Removes the Release Manager → VP Engineering delegation edge. The escalation chain (and its transitive closure) collapses to the unreachable tail. Demonstrates CQ6: delegation is a first-class edge, and the chain answer is its closure.

Scenarios.label: Move legal review under engineering
Scenarios.explanation: Re-owns the Legal Compliance role from the Legal dept to Engineering. With zero legal-owned steps, the cross-cutting 'involves engineering AND legal' check flips to No. Demonstrates CQ7: the boolean is an AND over department-owned step counts, derived from role ownership.

Scenarios.label: Detach the risk dataset
Scenarios.explanation: Clears the dataset's consumed-by-step link. The Q1 Risk Metrics dataset is no longer consumed by any step. Demonstrates CQ8: dataset consumption is an asserted relationship, and the answer is exactly which steps point at it.

CompetencyQuestions.questionText: What are all the steps in the release process, and in what order do they execute?
CompetencyQuestions.explanation: WorkflowSteps ordered by SequencePosition; the order is the transitive closure of the asserted StepPrecedence edges, materialized as ntwf:precedesStep.

CompetencyQuestions.questionText: Who or what is responsible for approving a production deployment?
CompetencyQuestions.explanation: The Release Approval Gate (step 3) -> its AssignedRole (Release Manager) -> FilledByHumanAgent. The role-agent indirection is resolved by the GateApproverHuman lookup.

CompetencyQuestions.questionText: Which steps are executed by AI agents, and which require human decision?
CompetencyQuestions.explanation: Each step's ExecutingAgentType (via its role's filler type) classifies AI vs pipeline vs human; RequiresHumanApproval marks the human-decision steps. CountAISteps aggregates the AI side.

CompetencyQuestions.questionText: What artifacts does the review produce, and what consumes them downstream?
CompetencyQuestions.expectedAnswer: 5 artifacts in a wasDerivedFrom chain: Risk Report -> Legal Clearance -> Release Authorization -> Deployment Log -> Post-Deployment Report
CompetencyQuestions.explanation: WorkflowArtifacts.ProducedByStep gives the producer; DerivedFromArtifact gives the PROV wasDerivedFrom parent; RequiredBySteps gives the downstream consumer.

CompetencyQuestions.questionText: Which workflows have not been reviewed or updated in twelve months?
CompetencyQuestions.expectedAnswer: No - Production Deployment is not stale (MonthsSinceModified 2 <= 12)
CompetencyQuestions.explanation: IsStale = MonthsSinceModified > StalenessThresholdMonths. The seed workflow was modified 2 months ago against a 12-month threshold, so it is not stale.

CompetencyQuestions.questionText: What happens when the VP of Engineering is unavailable during escalation?
CompetencyQuestions.expectedAnswer: Escalates Release Manager -> VP of Engineering -> Chief Technology Officer
CompetencyQuestions.explanation: Roles.DelegatesTo is a transitive chain. With the VP of Engineering unavailable, the next hop in the closure is the CTO.

CompetencyQuestions.questionText: Which workflows involve both Engineering and Legal?
CompetencyQuestions.expectedAnswer: Yes - Production Deployment involves both (Engineering-owned steps and Legal-owned steps)
CompetencyQuestions.explanation: InvolvesEngineeringAndLegal = AND(CountEngineeringOwnedSteps > 0, CountLegalOwnedSteps > 0). Step 2 is Legal-owned; steps 1/3/4/5 are Engineering-owned.

CompetencyQuestions.questionText: What datasets does the review consume, and which AI agents processed them?
CompetencyQuestions.expectedAnswer: Q1 2026 Risk Metrics, consumed by the AI Risk Assessment step and processed by RiskAnalysis-AI
CompetencyQuestions.explanation: Datasets.ConsumedBySteps (inverse of WorkflowSteps.ConsumesDataset) gives the consuming step; that step's role filler resolves the processing AI agent.

ScenarioCQEffects.note: Re-pointing prec-4-5 shrinks the precedence closure (10 → 7 pairs); positions recompute. No other answer moves.
Release Manager filled by AI → the gate has no human approver.
Same edit: the gate's step (3) is now AI-executed, so the AI-step count rises 2 → 3. Cannot be avoided — the gate role is a step executor.
Risk Analysis role handed to a human → AI-step count falls 2 → 1. Isolated.
Legal Clearance loses its wasDerivedFrom parent → the PROV chain splits. Isolated.
Modified date backdated past the window → IsStale flips true. Isolated.
Release Manager → VP edge removed → the escalation closure collapses. Isolated.
Legal role re-owned by Engineering → zero legal-owned steps → eng-AND-legal flips No. Isolated.
Dataset consumed-by link cleared → no step consumes it. Isolated.
Backdates the review date → IsStale true (while AI steps remain — the narrative risk).
Every AI/pipeline role reassigned to a human → AI-step count falls to 0.
Legal review step handed to AI → AI-step count rises 2 → 3 (the headline effect is the consistency-violation witness, not a CQ).

Every row and every column of WorkflowSteps must match the Postgres-oracle answer key on both engines.

Every row and every column of AIAgents must match the Postgres-oracle answer key on both engines.

Every row and every column of Scenarios must match the Postgres-oracle answer key on both engines.
The headline inference: 4 asserted edges entail the never-asserted 1→5 pair via transitive precedesStep.

ConformanceTests.displayName: Inferred delegation Release Manager to CTO
ConformanceTests.explanation: Escalation chain RM→VP Eng→CTO: the RM→CTO hop is never asserted; transitive delegatesTo entails it.
The multi-substrate equivalence claim: reasoner and Postgres views disagree on zero computed values.
Every row is a globally-addressable individual; the workflow's IRI is derived, stable, and identical on both engines.
Gate IRI derives through the step's path chain (lookup → calc chain).
Role→agent indirection: the step's executing agent type is derived through AssignedRole→FilledBy*.

ConformanceTests.displayName: Gate step executing agent type resolves
ConformanceTests.explanation: The approval-gate step resolves to its human Release Manager through the same one-edge chain.
COUNTIFS over the DERIVED IsExecutedByAI child field — conditional aggregation over a computed column.
The human side of the agent mix.
Boolean derived from the AI step count — an aggregation feeding a calc.

ConformanceTests.displayName: Gate step is human-filled at seed
ConformanceTests.explanation: requiresHumanApproval is satisfied at seed: the gate role is filled by a human.
The consistency rule does NOT fire on the seed facts — the clean baseline the mutation test breaks.

ConformanceTests.displayName: Workflow has no violation at seed
ConformanceTests.explanation: Workflow-level rollup of approval violations is false on seed facts.

ConformanceTests.displayName: Workflow status is a SKOS concept
ConformanceTests.explanation: workflowStatus points into the WorkflowStatusConcepts scheme.

ConformanceTests.displayName: Role capability is a SKOS concept
ConformanceTests.explanation: hasCapability points into the AgentCapabilityConcepts scheme.
Heuristic 6: label/comment documentation travels with the model into every substrate.
The gate's defining datatype property (raw, gate-only).
INDEX/MATCH lookup gate→step→role. KNOWN GAP CANDIDATE: both engines currently drop lookup columns from their individuals payload — this test exists to keep that gap visible until fixed.

ConformanceTests.displayName: Gate approver human resolves via lookup
ConformanceTests.explanation: Second-order lookup gate→role→human filler. Same known gap candidate as GateRole.

ConformanceTests.displayName: Release Manager has exactly one filler
ConformanceTests.explanation: Functional filledBy: exactly one of the three filler arms is set.

ConformanceTests.displayName: No roles with bad filler cardinality
ConformanceTests.explanation: Suite-4 cardinality witness: zero roles violate the exactly-one-filler rule on seed facts.

ConformanceTests.displayName: Release authorization derives from legal clearance
ConformanceTests.explanation: One link of the 5-artifact PROV wasDerivedFrom chain.

ConformanceTests.displayName: Risk report attributed to the risk AI
ConformanceTests.explanation: PROV attribution: the artifact names the AI agent that produced it.

ConformanceTests.displayName: Post-deploy report has a derivation parent
ConformanceTests.explanation: Boolean derived from the lineage link.
consumesDataset points at the dcat:Dataset; answers CQ8.
ownedBy chain step→role→department resolves to Legal.
Same chain resolving to Engineering.
The cross-department join CQ7 asks about.
The resolved SequencePosition (override-if-pinned, else PrecedingStepCount+1) computes identically in both substrates; step 4 has four transitive predecessors and no override, so it resolves to 4.
Drives the time-budget bar; raw datatype property.
4 asserted edges.
6 pairs exist only by inference (10-pair closure minus 4 asserted).
The 10-pair transitive closure, counted from the materialized closure rows.
Time-dependent calc (months granularity) — the staleness clock.
The CQ5 governance query on the seed facts.
The higher-compliance-risk join on the seed facts.
Steward vs Authority: only the Authority approves.
The other half of the governance split.
Semantic-versioning derivation from ChangeKind.
The minor release is back-compat by derivation.
Bitemporal validity: the open-ended assignment is current.

ConformanceTests.displayName: Assignment active as of audit date
ConformanceTests.explanation: The 'who filled this on the audit date' time-travel query.
AI→Human turnover detection across consecutive assignments.
The audit trigger derived from the agent-type change.
Workflow-level count of audit-triggering changes.
AI-system-registry blast radius: artifacts this AI produced.
Blast radius: workflows reachable from this AI's artifacts.
The audit anchor: which model version produced the attributed artifacts.
When the audited model version went live.

ConformanceTests.displayName: Assign AI to the human-only gate fires the violation
ConformanceTests.explanation: Suite-4 inject-an-error: filling the gate role with an AI must flip ApprovalConsistencyViolation on the step AND roll up to the workflow, on BOTH engines.

ConformanceTests.displayName: Backdating the review makes the workflow stale
ConformanceTests.explanation: Backdate dct:modified past the staleness window: IsStale must flip true.

ConformanceTests.displayName: Backdating fires the stale-AND-AI compliance join
ConformanceTests.explanation: Seed facts already include AI-executed steps, so staleness alone completes the IsStaleAndHasAIAgent join.

ConformanceTests.displayName: Dropping the sign-off requirement clears the violation
ConformanceTests.explanation: The violation is an AND of requiresHumanApproval and a non-human filler: with the requirement off, an AI filler is consistent.
